description: Armed Chinese robbers and bandits operating in the eastern Russia-China borderland from the second half of the 19th century to the first half of the 20th century. They were active in southeastern Siberia, the Russian Far East, and Manchuria. Many were former Chinese soldiers or unemployed laborers. They harassed Russian railway construction and fought in the Boxer Rebellion.

country_links.evidence_quote: Groups of honghuzi were recruited as guerrillas by the Imperial Japanese Army during the Russo-Japanese War of 1904-1905 into chunchu sabotage units.
country_links.context: Groups of Honghuzi were recruited as guerrillas by the Imperial Japanese Army during the Russo-Japanese War.

country_links.evidence_quote: operated in the areas of the eastern Russia - China borderland... harassed Russian efforts to build the Manchurian Railway... plagued the Russian troops in Manchuria... raided Russian settlers in the Far East region
country_links.context: The Honghuzi operated in eastern Russia, harassed Russian railway construction, attacked Russian settlers, and fought Russian troops.

time_period: Second half of 19th century - first half of 20th century
